name: Salmon-Spinach Party Dip
directions: Drain and flake salmon.
Combine flaked salmon with remaining ingredients, except vegetables and crackers.
Chill several hours to blend flavors.
Serve dip with vegetables and crackers.

name: Goat Cheese and Herb Dip
directions: Blend goat cheese, oil and yogurt in processor until smooth.
Transfer to small bowl.
Mix in the herbs.
Season dip to taste with salt and pepper.
Cover and refrigerate until dip is cold and flavors blend, about 3 hours.
(Can be made 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.) Serve dip with toasted baguette slices and raw vegetables.

similar.name: No Cook Pizza and Pasta Sauce

name: Vanilla Bean Hard Sauce
directions: Note: one 6 inch vanilla bean equals about 1 tablespoon extract,if you want to substitute extract for vanilla bean.
But using the vanilla bean seeds is so much more tasty!
Scrape tiny vanilla beads into sugar,stir well.
Combine with buter into mixing bowl.
Beat at medium speed of electric mixture until well blended.
Serve on Apple Berry Cobbler (another Recipezaar recipe),or any other fruit pie,pound cakes,etc.

name: Just the Best Barbecue Sauce Ever!
directions: Mix all ingredients in medium bowl.
Stir well.

name: Cranberry Relish
directions: Wash and scrub the apple and orange.
Rinse the cranberries in a colander.
Cut the ends off of the orange just a little, until the sections show through.
Core the apple and cut into quarters.
In a food processor on chop with chop blade, chop the frozen cranberries until a relish consistency.
Scoop out into a bowl.
Chop apple the same way and add to bowl.
Cut orange into quarters and add to processor-chop until all rind is in very small relish like particles.
Add to bowl.
Mix lightly and sprinkle about 1/2 or 2/3's of the one cup of sugar over mixture and let sit for about 1/2 hour.
After sitting, mix again and taste.
If mixture is too tart for you add more sugar until right for you.
We like it tart.
Add nuts last.
Note:We quadruple this recipe or more each year.
For each additional batch, keep out some of the sugar until the batch has rested.
The fruit does tend to sweeten some naturally and this shouldn't be real sweet.
Also-this freezes well.
After the holidays I make several more batches for the rest of the year and freeze in individual containers-ziplock type bowls- (one per meal 4 serving size).
Keeps forever and is handy to get out for picnics in the summer also.
****For freezing batches I do this without the nuts-they tend to mush out over long periods of time-and though they freeze well as nuts they don't adapt to freezing in liquid foods.

**2010 update re: option for nuts.  Place crushed nuts in a small side bowl and let those who like the nuts sprinkle what they want to mix in with their serving.  This also works well, because if there are leftovers, you can freeze it without worrying about the nuts getting mushy. Other option--make two batches, one with and one without<G> With large family gatherings and different tastes or food some can't eat, requirements, this solves the problem. Enjoy.

similar.name: Pear Mustard Glaze for meat & poultry

name: Basting Sauce for Ham
directions: Blend all the ingredients and use on baked or broiled ham, ham steaks, ham loaf or smoked pork chops.

similar.name: Just the Best Barbecue Sauce Ever!

name: Hollandaise Sauce
directions: Put egg yolks, lemon juice, vinegar, prepared mustard and pinch of cayenne pepper in a jug.
Beat until thick and creamy (a handheld blender is just great for this!).
Heat butter SLOWLY until melted and bubbly.
Remove from the heat and SLOWLY add to the egg yolk mixture.
Combine well.
(to absorb the butter into the egg yolk mixture).
Beat until smooth and thick.
Serve straight away (before the sauce loses it's heat)!

name: Easy Creme Fraiche
directions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the cream and buttermilk.
Cover the bowl with a towel and let stand in a warm place until thickened, about 12 hours.
When thickened, cover bowl tightly and refrigerate until well chilled, 2 to 3 hours.
The Creme Fraiche can be refrgerated, tightly covered for up to 2 weeks.

name: Cajun Glaze (good with ham)
directions: Blend all ingredients well and pour or brush over ham or other meat,good on ribbs too, prior to baking.
Baste meat occasionally with pan residue.

similar.name: Pear Mustard Glaze for meat & poultry

name: Sweetened Condensed Milk
directions: Mix the ingredients together.
Let sit 10 minutes before using.
Refrigerate any milk you don't use right away.

name: Canadian 'old-Time' Brandy Sauce
directions: Separate the eggs, and beat the egg yolks until thick and lemon coloured.
Slowly add 1/2 of the icing sugar, and beat constantly.
Beat egg whites until stiff.
Add remaining sugar- a little at a time, beating constantly.
Now add the beaten egg whites to the yolk mixture and stir in the brandy.

similar.name: Pear Mustard Glaze for meat & poultry

name: Brandy Cream Sauce
directions: Separate the eggs.
Beat the cream until soft peaks form.
Stir in the brandy (more if desired- according to taste).
Beat the 2 egg whites until stiff.
Gradually beat in the caster sugar- a little at a time.
Continue to beat until all the sugar has dissolved.
Now, beat in the egg yolks, and lastly fold in the brandy cream!

similar.name: Pear Mustard Glaze for meat & poultry

name: My Mom's Pizza Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Do not cook.
Stores well.
Freezes well.

similar.name: No Cook Pizza and Pasta Sauce

name: Tartar Sauce
directions: Combine ingredients; mix thoroughly.
CHILL.

name: Homemade Sweetened Condensed Milk
directions: Combine all the ingredients.
Whip until thick.
Store in fridge until needed.

name: Dijon Mustard Butter
directions: Mix everything together until fluffy.
Serve.

similar.name: Pear Mustard Glaze for meat & poultry

name: (Shrimp) Cocktail Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients and chill.
When ready to serve dip shrimp and enjoy.

name: Honey Mustard-Dill Dipping Sauce
directions: In a small bowl stir together all ingredients.
Serve.

similar.name: Copy Cat Wendy's Honey Mustard Dipping Sauce

name: Almond Butter Icing
directions: Cream together the shortening and the butter, add the almond extract and beat until fluffy.
Beat in the confectioner's sugar, one cup at a time.
Add the milk alternately with the confectioner's sugar, one tbs at a time until the icing reaches the desired consistency.
Color and decorate as you like!

name: Sugar and Spice Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Serve chilled with fruit for dipping.

name: Fiery Horseradish Sauce
directions: Thinly slice onion and place in a small bowl.
Stir in mayonnaise, 2 tbsp horseradish, parsley& Dijon if using.
Mix well.
Taste and stir in remaining horseradish, 1 tsp at a time, if you like.
This sauce can be made up to 3 days before serving.

name: Horseradish Cream Sauce
directions: In a chilled bowl, using electric mixer, whip the cream until it forms soft peaks.
In separate bowl, combine mayonnaise, horseradish and mustard.
Using a rubber spatula, fold in the whipped cream.
Add the sugar, and salt and pepper to taste.
Stir well; transfer to serving bowl.

name: Tartar Sauce
directions: Mix in blender.

name: Basil and Garlic Mayo
directions: Blend first three ingredients in food processor until basil is finely chopped.
Add mayonnaise.
Enjoy on your favorite sandwich!

name: Orange-Ginger Mayonnaise
directions: Whisk together all ingredients and allow to sit covered in refrigerator to blend flavors.

name: Creole Mustard

name: Finadene Sauce
directions: Pour the vinegar or lemon juice into a bowl.
Add soy sauce a little bit at a time to mix it according to your taste.
Add the onions and peppers.
Chill until ready to serve.
(You may use a hot sauce instead of hot peppers if you prefer. Use either, adjusting amounts to your preference for"heat!").

name: Cocktail Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Chill.

name: Amatuer Salsa
directions: Mix all ingredients in small glass bowl and chill.
You may use more or less of nay on the ingredients to suit your taste.
this seems to get"hotter" after a day or two in the fridge.

name: Cucumber Sauce for Fish & Seafood
directions: Mix the cuke with the sour cream, miracle whip, chives, lemon juice, salt& pepper Mix well and chill until serving with hot or cold seafood.

name: "Rose Tobacco" (Oregon Trail Rose Candy)
directions: Alternate layers of brown sugar and rose petals in the jelly jar, beginning with brown sugar.
Pour the brandy over, put the lid on, and put it in the fridge for at least a couple of months.
I don't recall if the gift shop version had brandy or other liquid added into the jar, but I believe that my having used liquid worked very well and I wouldn't make the rose tobacco without it.

name: Fennel Seed Mustard
directions: Mix fennel seed in your food processor until crushed.
Add mustard, brown sugar, vinegar,& salt, mix well.
With the machine running very slowly add the oil in a steady stream.
Blend until it has the consistency of mayonaisse.
Transfer to a jar and keep in the fridge.

name: Thai Dragon Salsa
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.
Serve chilled.
Serving suggestion: goes well with soft spring rolls, crispy spring rolls, and chilled steamed shrimp.

name: Veronica's Sour Cream Horseradish Fondue Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl and mix well.

name: Not Yet Another Barbecue Sauce
directions: Add all ingredients to a food processor.
Pulse until smooth.
Use for basting during the last 3-5 minutes of grilling.
Have a nice time inventing other interesting uses of this zesty sauce.

similar.name: Just the Best Barbecue Sauce Ever!

name: Honey Surprise
directions: Put the honey in a pretty jar of your choice.  Add the cinnamon, cloves, and allspice.  Set the jar in a bowl of hot water for 20 minutes for the flavors to develop.
Put lid on jar and attach a wide ribbon around lid with a wooden honey server to serve the honey.

name: Tartar Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients: cover tightly and refrigerate for an hour or so for flavors to blend.

name: Thousand Island Dressing
directions: Mix ingredients together and blend well.
Cover and refrigerate.

name: Cocktail Sauce
directions: Mix the ingredients well and let sit in refrigerator until ready to serve.
Making this at least an hour ahead will let the flavors mix.
Can be stored in a covered container for a week.

name: Tartar Sauce
directions: Mix all together in the food processor and pulse for about 10 seconds until finely chopped, but not smooth.
Best if refrigerated for an hour before use.
Note: Can be made without the food processor, using dill relish or finely minced pickle.
Lasts quite a while in the refrigerator.

name: Marmalade Sauce for Shrimp or Calamari (Just like Outback)
directions: Combine all ingredients and blend well.
Can be used immediately, or refrigerate for several hours for flavors to blend.

name: Chili Sauce Substitute
directions: mix together.

name: Horseradish Cream
directions: In small bowl, mix first 4 ingredients.
Chill.
Before serving, stir in the chives.

name: black bean salsa- too good and easy
directions: combine all ingredients, serve with taco chips.

name: Ed's Seafood Cocktail Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients and chill.
Use with fresh, cooked shrimp or any other seafood that you like with a spicy sauce.

name: Homemade Creme Fraiche
directions: In a bowl, combine the cream and buttermilk or lemon juice.
Cover with a clean kitchen cloth in a warm, draft free place and let sit until thickened, but still pourable consistency, 12 to 16 hours.
Stir and refrigerate until ready to use.
(can be refrigerated up to 1 week.).

name: Seafood Cocktail Sauce
directions: Whip cream lightly.
Combine remaining ingredients, then fold whipped cream through the sauce gently.

name: black bean salsa

stir in remaining ingredients.
season with salt& pepper.
serve with tortilla chips.

similar.name: black bean salsa- too good and easy

name: Tartar Sauce with Horseradish
directions: Combine all ingredients and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
The sauce will keep in the fridge for about 3-5 days.
Serve with fish.

name: Pear Mustard Glaze for meat & poultry
directions: Place all the ingredients is a food processor, with steel blade, process until you have a very smooth terxture.

name: Mississippi White Salsa Dip
directions: In a medium bowl, combine mayonnaise and sour cream.
Add lime juice, garlic, cilantro, black olives, scallions, hot pepper sauce, salt and pepper.
Taste and adjust seasonings if desired.
Chill until serving.
Serve with blue corn tortilla chips.

name: Doughnut Glaze
directions: Soften 1/2 pkg of gelatin with 2 tablespoons of cold water.
Add boiling water and then add the powdered sugar; mix till it is smooth.

name: Brandy Butter
directions: Whip butter in a small bowl with electric beaters until light and fluffy.
Gradudually add icing sugar and brandy, beat until smooth, Refrigerate 1 hour, or until firm.

name: Remoulade Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl; mix until well blended.
Refrigerate several hours or overnight.
Good served with shrimp, crabmeat, lobster or tomatoes.
Makes 1 1/2 cups.

name: Vegetarian Pesto Sauce
directions: Blend or process in food processor until creamy.

similar.name: No Cook Pizza and Pasta Sauce

name: Carol's Horseradish Cream
directions: Whip the whipping cream til soft peaks form.
Combine mayo, horseradish and mustard and fold into the whipped cream.
Add sugar and black pepper.
Makes 2 cups.

name: The Worlds Best Barbecue Sauce
directions: Mix ketchup, vinegar, mustard, Worcestershire sauce, barbecue sauce, hot sauce, cayenne pepper, garlic, allspice, parsley, onion, and sugar together well.
Best if chilled overnight.

similar.name: Just the Best Barbecue Sauce Ever!

name: Creamy Wasabi-mustard Seafood Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.

name: Brown Sugar-lemon Cocktail Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.
Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours before serving.

name: Green Olive & Garlic Mojo
directions: Combine all ingredients and allow to sit for at least an hour.
This doesn't have a long shelf life; eat within a day or so (it's not tough to do!).

name: 1-2-3 Black Bean Salsa Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl and mix well.
Refrigerate for several hours to blend flavors.
Serve with tortilla chips.

similar.name: black bean salsa- too good and easy

name: Blue Cheese Dip/ Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Enjoy.

name: Woodland White Sauce
directions: Mix well.
Store in the refrigerator.

name: Low-fat Heavy Cream Substitute
directions: Combine and in a blender.
puree for 5 minutes.

name: Pesto
directions: Place all ingredients in a mini-chopper or blender and blend until smooth.

name: My Secret Shrimp Sauce Recipe
directions: In a blender or food processor, add all the ingredients.
Blend until smooth.
Store in a glass jar with lid.
Chill for a hour or overnight before serving.
Keep in refrigerator up to 2 weeks.
NOTE: If you want it to be alittle more of a hotter taste, add alittle more Tabasco sauce and another dash of white pepper.

name: Homemade Honey Mustard

name: Bourbon-peach Barbecue Sauce

name: No Cook Pizza and Pasta Sauce
directions: Put all the ingredients in a large bowl and whisk them together.

Refrigerate when finished or make a larger batch and freeze it for another time.

name: Sassy Salsa Dip
directions: Blend yogurt and salsa.
Stir in optional ingredients (if you want).

name: " Home-Churned" Butter
directions: Allow 2 1/2 lbs. butter to come to room temperature for about 1-2 hours.
Beat with electric mixer, adding 1/2 T. salt, 2 cups buttermilk until light and fluffy.
Keep refrigerated in a covered container.

name: Prawn (Shrimp) Butter
directions: Chop the prawns and anchovies into small pieces, then pound in a mortar and pestle.

name: Shrimp Sauce (Like Cocktail Sauce)
directions: Mix all ingredients.  Use right away or refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Classic New Orleans Seafood Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients with a fork and keep refrigerated until using.
It will keep for a week or so.

name: Easy Caesar Dressing
directions: Mix all in a small bowl until creamy.
See? simple!

similar.name: No Cook Pizza and Pasta Sauce

name: Yogurt-Cucumber Sauce
directions: Using a melon baller or small spoon, scoop out the seedy center of the cucumber.  Coarsely shred the cucumber.  Squeeze the excess liquid from the shredded cucumber without mashing it.
In a large bowl, using the back of a spoon, mash the garlic with the salt to a paste.  (This can also be done on your cutting board with a knife).
Stir in the yogurt, olive oil and mint.  Add the shredded cucumber, season with salt and pepper and serve.
This sauce can be refrigerated overnight.

name: Falafel Yogurt Sauce
directions: Blend all ingredients together!
Enjoy :).

name: Garlic Aioli
directions: Whisk ingredients together.

name: Cilantro Cocktail Sauce
directions: Combine ketchup and horseradish.
Stir until well incorporated.
Add Worcestershire, hot sauce, salt and pepper; taste, and season according to preferences.
NOTE: to add a little extra kick, sprinkle a dash of cayenne pepper and mix well.
FOR EASE: combine favorite premade cocktail sauce with chopped fresh cilantro.

name: Ma's Bulk Barbecue Sauce
directions: Stir in all ingredients together.

similar.name: Just the Best Barbecue Sauce Ever!

name: Goat Cheese Aioli
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Use as a yummy dip/spread for spicy shrimp or steak!

name: Pickapeppa Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bottle and refrigerate for at least a week to blend all the flavors.
Shake at least once a day.

name: Aioli - Jamie Oliver
directions: Grind the garlic and salt in a mortar and pestle (or chop the garlic finely and rub the salt into it with the flat of your chef's knife, smearing it across your cutting board several times).
Place egg yolk and mustard in a bowl and whisk. Whisking vigorously, add olive oil drop by drop. Once about 1/2 cup of oil has been added, you can start pouring faster, still whisking continuously.
When all the oil is incorporated, add garlic, lemon juice, salt and pepper.
You can also add additional accent flavors, such as basil, fennel fronds, dill, or chopped roasted nuts.

name: Roasted Red Pepper Sauce for Crab Cakes
directions: Combine all ingredients, except for the mayonnaise, salt and pepper,  in a the bowl of a food processor and blend until smooth.
Add mayonnaise and process until combined.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Icing Drizzle
directions: Combine the powdered sugar and half and half or cream and stir until the sugar is completely absorbed by the cream. It should look just like the packaged icing that comes in store-bought ready-made cinnamon rolls.

Add the teaspoon of vanilla extract. You can also add a few drops of almond extract, but be careful--this extract is very strong! Recipes often recommend a few drops per CAN of cheesecake fruit topping, etc. This is only half a cup of icing, so don't add too much!
The icing is ready to drizzle over scones, sweet biscuits, etc. I drizzle on the icing with a small spoon in a zig-zag pattern.

name: Sour Cream Mustard Dipping Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients and serve.  I guess the taste will be better if it's prepared a few hours early and kept in the fridge.

similar.name: Copy Cat Wendy's Honey Mustard Dipping Sauce

name: Tartar Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients together well.
Refrigerate at least one hour before serving.

name: Blender Remoulade Sauce
directions: Blend all ingredients except for the green onions or chives, for 5-6 seconds.
Add the green onions or chives and now blend only 2 seconds.  Do not blend longer or you will have puree.
Chill overnight.
Serve with shrimp arranged on a bed of lettuce on individual salad plates.

similar.name: Homemade Basic Mayonnaise Top Chef Spike Mendelsohn

name: Horseradish Steak Sauce
directions: Mix all together and blend with a hand blender until smooth.
Serve with your favorite steak.

name: Purple Basil Pesto
directions: Mix all ingredients except the olive oil in a food processor until thoroughly combined and the consistency you like.
With the food processor running, drizzle in the olive oil through that chute thingie. Add more salt to taste if necessary.
Store pesto in the refrigerator for several days or freeze.

name: Hoisin and Mustard Fondue Sauce
directions: In a bowl, mix all ingredients with a whisk.  Add salt and pepper.

name: Toasted Sesame Fondue Sauce
directions: In a bowl, mix all ingredients with a whisk.

similar.name: Veronica's Sour Cream Horseradish Fondue Dip

name: Fresh Basil Pesto
directions: Combine the basil in with the pine nuts, pulse a few times in a food processor. (If you are using walnuts instead of pine nuts and they are not already chopped, pulse them a few times first, before adding the basil.) Add the garlic, pulse a few times more.

Slowly add the olive oil in a constant stream while the food processor is on. Stop to scrape down the sides of the food processor with a rubber spatula. Add the grated cheese and pulse again until blended. Add a pinch of salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.
Makes 1 cup.
Serve with pasta, or over baked potatoes, or spread over toasted baguette slices.

similar.name: No Cook Pizza and Pasta Sauce

name: Jalapeno Balsamic Vinegar
directions: place the jalapeno slices into a wide mouthed jar and cover with the vinegar.
seal and allow to sit for 1 week then serve as a table condiment.

name: Cristmas Hummus
directions: puree the beans with the paprika,they don't have to be completely smooth;in fact a little texture is a good thing.
in a bowl mix the bean with the remaining ingredients.
serve with toasted pita wedges.

name: Garlic Sauce
directions: Blend the first three ingredients until smooth, then stir through the last two mixing well.
Refrigerate about an hour to allow the flavours to develop.

similar.name: No Cook Pizza and Pasta Sauce

name: Zingerman's Russian Dressing (For Ruben Sandwiches)
directions: Combine all ingredients and whisk together.

name: Twisted Tartar Sauce (French Polynesia)
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.

name: Homemade Basic Mayonnaise Top Chef Spike Mendelsohn
directions: Add the eggs, mustard, vinegar, and salt to a food processor or blender. Process for 30 seconds in the food processor, or for 10 seconds in the blender. With the motor running, drizzle in the oil slowly at first, then add in a thin, steady stream until all the oil is added and the mixture is smooth. Stop the motor and taste.
If the sauce is too thick, thin it with a little hot water. If too thin, process a little longer. The mayonnaise can be refrigerated in an airtight container for up to 1 week.

name: Deli Mayonnaise (Recipe With Tutorial)
directions: Select the method of blending: blender (not the easiest but workable), food processor (my personal favorite), mixer with whisk attachment, or by hand (for the purist who has a strong arm).
Combine salt & dry mustard in a small bowl and reserve.  In a separate bowl, combine the vinegar and lemon juice and reserve.
Blend (or process or whisk --  you get my meaning) the egg yolks until smooth and slightly lighter in color.
Add the salt/dry mustard mixture.  Blend smooth.
Add about 1/3 of the vinegar/lemon juice.  Blend smooth (This stage is the "sabayon" of the mayonnaise, or the base before the emulsion is begun.).

While blending (about half speed on the blender or higher speed by the other methods), drizzle in about 1/4 tsp of the oil.  Blend until the oil has completely incorporated and can't be seen any more.  Repeat several times.  (This is establishing your "emulsion", or placing the oil in suspension in the sauce.).

While blending, begin drizzling the oil into the bowl at a SLOW but steady rate --  not more than a teaspoon every couple of seconds.  If the mixture begins to sound "wet" or looks curdled, stop adding oil and allow the mixture to emulsify completely before continuing.  (You do NOT need to pour the oil directly onto the moving whisk/blades.  Pouring down the side of the the bowl works fine IF YOU ARE PATIENT.)  Continue until 1/3 of the oil is emulsified.  Note:  if the mixture continues to appear curdled and will not mix together successfully after 30 seconds of blending without adding additional oil, you likely have a "broken" mayonnaise, and need to take steps to fix it before continuing --  a process not covered here.
Add about 1/2 the remaining vinegar/lemon juice and blend until incorporated.
Repeat the previous 2 steps.  You can add oil at a faster rate, but a slow, steady drizzle will still be the safest.  Add the last of the vinegar/lemon juice at the end and blend smooth.
Add the last of the oil as before until it is all incorporated and emulsified.  Make sure to scrape your bowl/blending container before giving it a final blending.  May be used immediately.
Store refrigerated in an airtight container.
Note: any recipe that uses uncooked egg can be considered a health hazard.  If you are unsure about this, we recommend using pasteurized egg yolk.

similar.name: Homemade Basic Mayonnaise Top Chef Spike Mendelsohn
Copy Cat Wendy's Honey Mustard Dipping Sauce

name: Zhoug
directions: Add parsley, cilantro, garlic, chiles, shallot and olive oil to a bowl.
Toast cumin, coriander and peppercorns in dry sauté pan until fragrant but not smoking.
Cool then add to spice grinder and turn into a powder.
Add powder, salt and cardamom to sauce.
Stir in enough olive oil to make a thick chunky green salsa.
Add the mixture to the food processor and pulse until the mixture is mixed well and coarse in texture.
Taste, and reseason as needed.

name: Copy Cat Wendy's Honey Mustard Dipping Sauce
directions: Mix ingredients together until smooth.
You can eat immediately, or refrigerate for several hours to allow flavors to blend.
